characters.name: Barbara "Barb" Henrickson
characters.description: Barbara HenricksonBarbara HenricksonAliasesBarbBarbara Dutton (maiden name)RelativesNancy Dutton(mother)Cindy Dutton Price(sister)Bill Henrickson(husband)Sarah Henrickson(daughter)Ben Henrickson(son)Tancy Henrickson(daughter)William Quittman(grandson)Scott Quittman(son-in-law)Nicolette "Nicki" Grant(sister wife)Margene Heffman(sister wife)AffiliationHenrickson familyOccupationSubstitute teacherGuidance counselorFormer homemakerBiographical informationMarital statusSpiritually married toBill Henrickson(first wife)Date of birthLate 1950s or early 1960sPlace of birthUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleHeightApprox. 5'8"Eye colorBlueAppearancesPortrayed byJeanne TripplehornDebutPilotBarbara "Barb" Henricksonis the first wife of Bill Henrickson and the moral and emotional anchor of the Henrickson family. Raised in a traditional Mormon household, Barb initially struggles with her husband's decision to embrace polygamy. Over time, she navigates the complexities of plural marriage, balancing her personal beliefs, identity, and her devotion to family. Thoughtful, composed, and deeply spiritual, Barb often finds herself torn between her independence and her role within the unconventional family structure. Her evolution throughout the series reflects a woman redefining faith, womanhood, and her place in a changing world. Barbara "Barb" Henrickson was born into a devout mainstream LDS family in Utah. Raised with traditional values, she lived a conventional Mormon life and eventually married Bill Henrickson, whom she met while attending college. Their early marriage was monogamous and rooted in shared faith and family ideals. Barb’s life changed dramatically when she was diagnosed with cancer after the birth of their third child. During her illness, fearing for her family’s future, she agreed to let Bill take a second wife—Nicolette "Nicki" Grant—whom she chose herself. This decision, made from a place of love and sacrifice, gradually led to a full embrace of plural marriage, though not without deep emotional cost.

characters.name: Ben Henrickson
characters.description: Ben HenricksonAliasesBennyRelativesBill Henrickson(father)Barbara Henrickson(mother)Sarah Henrickson(older sister)Tancy Henrickson(younger sister)Nicolette "Nicki" Grant(stepmother/sister wife)Margene "Margie" Heffman(stepmother/sister wife)Wayne Henrickson (half-brother)Raymond Henrickson (half-brother)Aaron Heffman (half-brother)Lester Heffman (half-brother)Adaleen Grant (step-grandmother)Roman Grant (step-grandfather)AffiliationHenrickson familyPhysical descriptionGenderMaleHeightApprox. 6'0"AppearancesDebutPilotBen Henricksonis the eldest son of Bill and Barb Henrickson, and the first child raised entirely within the family's polygamous lifestyle. Thoughtful, earnest, and often caught between competing values, Ben embodies the generational conflict that definesBig Love. He is deeply influenced by his father’s spiritual beliefs, but also haunted by the emotional strain those beliefs place on the family. As Ben comes of age, he grapples with questions of faith, identity, masculinity, and legacy—wondering whether he’s meant to follow in his father's footsteps or forge his own path. Ben’s evolution across the series charts a boy growing up under the weight of a father’s towering ideals and a mother’s quiet moral compass. Eager to please but often lost, Ben’s inner conflict becomes more pronounced as he tries to reconcile teenage desires with a complex religious framework. His trajectory mirrors the show’s larger themes of belief, control, and the cost of choosing your own truth. <Text Here>

characters.name: Bill Henrickson
characters.description: Bill HenricksonBill HenricksonAliasesWilliam Orville HenricksonBillRelativesLois Henrickson(mother)Frank Harlow(father)Joey Henrickson(brother)Wanda Henrickson(sister-in-law)Sarah Henrickson(daughter)Ben Henrickson(son)Tancy Henrickson(daughter)Lester & Wayne Grant(sons)Aaron & Lester Heffman(sons)AffiliationHenrickson familyOccupationOwner of Henrickson Home PlusFounder of a breakaway Mormon churchBusinessmanBiographical informationMarital statusLegally married to:Barbara "Barb" Henrickson(first wife)Spiritually married to:Nicolette "Nicki" Grant(second wife)Margene "Margie" Heffman(third wife)Ana Marković(fourth wife; briefly married- quickly divorced)Date of birth1960sPlace of birthUtah, United StatesDate of deathWhere Men and Mountains MeetPlace of deathUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionSpecieshumanGenderMaleHeightApprox. 6'0"Eye colorBrownAppearancesPortrayed byBill PaxtonDebutPilot Spiritually married to: Bill Henricksonis the central figure ofBig Loveand patriarch of the Henrickson family. Raised in a polygamist sect but exiled as a teen, Bill builds a successful life in mainstream society while maintaining his commitment to plural marriage. Charismatic, ambitious, and deeply conflicted, Bill juggles the demands of his expanding family, his business empire, and a personal spiritual vision. His journey explores the tension between faith, power, and personal legacy, ultimately leading him down a path of both triumph and tragedy.

characters.name: Margene "Margie" Heffman
characters.description: Margene "Margie" HeffmanAliasesMargieMargene HenricksonRelativesBill Henrickson(spiritual husband)Barbara "Barb" Henrickson(sister wife)Nicolette "Nicki" Grant(sister wife)Aaron & Lester Heffman (sons)Nell Heffman (daughter)Ginger Heffman (mother)Maxine Heffman (grandmother)AffiliationHenrickson familyOccupationSales associate (formerly)TV home shopping hostEntrepreneurBiographical informationMarital statusSpiritually married to Bill Henrickson (third wife)Date of birthEarly 1980sPlace of birthUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderFemaleHeightApprox. 5'6"Eye colorBlueAppearancesPortrayed byGinnifer GoodwinDebutPilot Margene "Margie" Heffmanis the youngest ofBill Henrickson’s three wives. Bubbly, impulsive, and idealistic, Margene enters the family as a naive 20-something new to polygamy, but gradually grows into a more confident and independent woman. Her journey explores identity, belonging, and the tension between youthful freedom and family responsibility. Despite her inexperience, Margene brings warmth and emotional honesty to the Henrickson household, often acting as a bridge between her more traditional sister wives. Margene "Margie" Heffman joined the Henrickson family as Bill's third wife when she was only in her early twenties. Raised in a chaotic, non-religious household by a single mother and an often-absent grandmother, Margene found herself drifting through life with little guidance or purpose. While working as a babysitter for Bill and Barb’s children, she was drawn into their plural lifestyle—eventually accepting Bill’s marriage proposal despite having little understanding of what being a sister wife truly entailed.

characters.name: Nicolette "Nicki" Grant
characters.description: Nicolette GrantNicolette "Nicki" GrantAliasesNickiNicolette HenricksonNicolette Grant HenricksonRelativesRoman Grant(father)Adaleen Grant (mother)Bill Henrickson(husband)Wayne Henrickson (son)Raymond Henrickson (son)Cara Lynn Walker (daughter)Alby Grant(brother)Barbara Henrickson(sister wife)Margene Heffman(sister wife)AffiliationHenrickson familyJuniper Creek (formerly)OccupationGovernment employee (fraudulent identity)HomemakerFamily securityBiographical informationMarital statusSpiritually married toBill Henrickson(second wife)Date of birthLate 1970sPlace of birthUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleHeightApprox. 5'5"Eye colorHazelAppearancesPortrayed byChloë SevignyDebutPilotNicolette "Nicki" Grantis the second wife of Bill Henrickson and the daughter ofRoman Grant, the powerful prophet of the Juniper Creek compound. Raised in a rigid and deeply patriarchal polygamist sect, Nicki is devout, secretive, and often manipulative — fiercely loyal to her roots yet deeply conflicted by life in the modern world. She struggles with her identity, marriage, and motherhood while clinging to her beliefs and family legacy. Complex, volatile, and emotionally guarded, Nicki’s journey is one of battling control and surrender — both to faith and love — while protecting the fragile world she helps hold together. Nicolette Grant was born and raised in the rigid, patriarchal environment of the Juniper Creek compound, under the strict rule of her father, Roman Grant, the prophet of their polygamist sect. From an early age, Nicki was deeply immersed in the religious doctrines and customs of her community, which shaped her worldview and sense of identity. Married young to Bill Henrickson as his second wife, Nicki struggles to reconcile her upbringing with the more modern and secular lifestyle she encounters outside the compound. Her marriage is marked by loyalty and duty, but also by internal conflict and jealousy as she navigates the complexities of polygamy within the Henrickson family.

characters.name: Roman Grant
characters.description: Roman GrantRoman GrantAliasesProphetLeader of Juniper CreekRelativesAdaleen Grant (wife)Nicolette "Nicki" Grant (daughter)Albert "Alby" Grant (son)Numerous other wives and childrenCara Lynn Walker (granddaughter)Wayne Henrickson (grandson)Raymond Henrickson (grandson)Numerous other grandchildrenAffiliationJuniper Creek CompoundOccupationProphet, Religious leaderBiographical informationMarital statusPolygamist, multiple wives (includingAdaleen Grant)Place of birthUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionGenderMaleHeightApprox. 6'Eye colorDark brownAppearancesPortrayed byMichael HoganDebutPilotStatusStatusDeadRoman Grantis the powerful, charismatic, and ruthless prophet of the Juniper Creek compound, a fundamentalist Mormon polygamist sect. As the patriarchal leader, he wields enormous influence over his followers and controls the compound’s resources with an iron fist. Roman is a complex antagonist whose deep-seated need for control masks vulnerabilities shaped by years of harsh religious tradition. His relationship with his family and enemies is marked by manipulation, loyalty, and the ongoing battle between tradition and modernity. Roman Grant is the long-standing prophet and leader of the Juniper Creek polygamist compound, a fundamentalist Mormon sect in Utah. Over decades, he has built his power through strict religious authority, controlling not only his vast family but also the wider compound community. Known for his charismatic yet ruthless leadership style, Roman enforces conservative, patriarchal values and polygamy as core to his identity and rule. Roman’s early life is marked by traditional upbringing within polygamist culture, learning the ways of the faith and the importance of maintaining control over family and followers. As prophet, he commands multiple wives and many children, using his influence to wield significant political and social power.

characters.name: Sarah Henrickson
characters.description: Sarah Elisabeth HenricksonSarah HenricksonAliasesSarahSarah Henrickson QuittmanRelativesWilliam Quittman (son, viaScott Scott Quittman)Scott Quittman(husband)Bill Henrickson(father)Barbara Henrickson(mother)Ben Henrickson(younger brother)Tancy "Teeny" Henrickson (younger sister)Wayne Henrickson (half-brother)Raymond Henrickson (half-brother)Aaron Heffman (half-brother)Lester Heffman (half-brother)Nell Heffman (half-sister)Nicolette "Nicki" Grant(stepmother/sister wife)Margene "Margie" Heffman(stepmother/sister wife)Adaleen Grant (step-grandmother)Roman Grant (step-grandfather)AffiliationHenrickson familyJuniper Creek (formerly)OccupationHigh school student (formerly/graduated)College student (currently)MotherBiographical informationMarital statusMarried to Scott (husband)Date of birthLate 1980sPlace of birthUtah, United StatesPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleHeightApprox. 5'7"Eye colorBlueAppearancesPortrayed byAmanda SeyfriedDebutPilotSarah Henricksonis the eldest daughter ofBill HenricksonandBarbara Henrickson. Intelligent, strong-willed, and compassionate, Sarah often finds herself at odds with her family’s polygamous lifestyle. Throughout the series, she grapples with her faith, independence, and the complexities of growing up in a non-traditional household. As she matures, Sarah seeks to forge her own path, balancing love, education, and a desire to live life on her own terms while still maintaining ties to her family. Sarah Henrickson is the eldest child of Bill and Barbara Henrickson. Growing up in a secretive polygamous household, Sarah often struggled with the moral and emotional complexities of her family’s lifestyle. Intelligent, sensitive, and independent-minded, she frequently clashed with her parents—especially Bill—over religion, autonomy, and the expectations placed on her as the oldest daughter. Sarah felt alienated from the family’s religious beliefs and rejected the Principle of plural marriage altogether. Her rebellion wasn't reckless, but deeply rooted in a desire for honesty, normalcy, and control over her own life. Over the series, Sarah matures into a thoughtful young woman, driven by values of authenticity and compassion. Her journey culminates in her choosing to leave the compound life behind and move forward with a future of her own making.
